methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting, preparing, and analyzing pollen samples from the subfamily Malvoideae using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM) to study their morphological characteristics.

steps.description: Pollen grains were obtained from material fixed in 70% ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen grains fixed in 70% ethanol

steps.description: Samples were analyzed using standard methods (acetolysis) described by Erdtman (1966).
The pollen was studied by light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen samples using LM and SEM

steps.description: For LM, the pollen grains were measured for 30 grains from each individual.
steps.searchable_summary: measure 30 pollen grains per individual using LM

steps.description: For the observation by SEM, the pollen grains were dried, coated with a sputter coater and observed with a scanning electron microscope, JEOL: JSM 6460 LV.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples for SEM by drying and sputter coating
